commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=a53d46dd3325428dfa7324f5733c9a00f6b354de)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Matrix progress rendering to default command progress to status-only lifecycle text, adds Matrix `commandText` config acceptance, updates shared formatter tests, and documents the Matrix raw opt-in.

PR surface: Source +52, Tests +91, Docs +1. Total +144 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence path: Codex commandExecution progress can be carried through suppressed item progress plus `onToolStart`, and PR head still renders command tool-start status mode as the opaque tool label.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining code gap is narrow, but insufficient real Matrix proof and the compatibility-sensitive default flip make this a human-review path rather than an automated repair marker.

target.title: fix(matrix): neutralize command progress failure labels
target.body:
## Summary
- hide raw command/exec progress text in Matrix draft progress notices by default
- replace the old opaque `Exec` status-only default with neutral lifecycle text (`Running command` / `Command finished`) so command-progress artifacts do not show false/problematic `failed` wording
- add Matrix `streaming.progress.commandText` and `streaming.preview.commandText` config support so trusted debug rooms can opt back into raw command details
- add Matrix monitor/schema regression coverage, shared streaming coverage, and document the safer default

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: a private Matrix room displayed an internal command-progress notice for a GitHub PR edit operation that appeared to say the operation `failed`. This was not a normal assistant reply. It was a Matrix command-progress/status artifact that both exposed raw command text and presented a false/problematic failure-looking state to the user.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on macOS using the patched PR head `a53d46dd33`; the command below ran the real patched command-progress formatter with Matrix streaming config objects.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm -s exec tsx -e '<redacted inline probe importing ./src/channels/streaming.ts>'`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): copied terminal output from the patched checkout:

```json
{
  "defaultRunningLine": "🛠️ Running command",
  "defaultFailedLine": "🛠️ Command finished",
  "defaultContainsRaw": false,
  "defaultContainsFailed": false,
  "rawLine": "🛠️ gh pr edit 89920 --body explicit-raw-command",
  "rawContainsCommand": true,
  "rawContainsDetail": true
}
```

- Observed result after fix: default Matrix command progress no longer renders raw command text, private command arguments, or the literal failure label. Running command progress renders `Running command`; a terminal command-progress event, even if internally tagged `failed`, renders neutral `Command finished` rather than a user-visible failure assertion. An explicit `commandText: "raw"` configuration still renders the raw command for trusted debug rooms.
- What was not tested: a full live Matrix gateway room delivery was not run from this external PR branch.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the proof uses a local patched checkout and synthetic non-identifying command text. Live private room identifiers, message identifiers, exact command body, local path, branch/worktree path, transcript text, and agent names are intentionally omitted.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): live private room observation showed a command-progress notice equivalent to `gh pr edit <PR_NUMBER> --repo <ORG>/<REPO> --body ...` with a failure-looking status; private identifying details are redacted.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:
- `pnpm -s exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-matrix.config.ts extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/handler.test.ts -t "hides raw command text in Matrix progress previews by default|allows Matrix progress previews to opt into raw command text"` — 2 passed, 113 skipped
- `pnpm -s exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts -t "formats progress draft lines" --reporter verbose` — 1 passed, 28 skipped
- `pnpm -s exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-matrix.config.ts extensions/matrix/src/config-schema.test.ts -t "accepts Matrix streaming preview tool progress config"` — 1 passed, 9 skipped
- `git diff --check`
- Previous branch validation retained from earlier PR revision: `pnpm -s tsgo:prod`, `pnpm -s check:test-types`, `pnpm -s test:extensions:package-boundary`, `pnpm -s tsgo:core:test`, `pnpm -s lint:core`

Regression coverage added/updated:
- Matrix monitor tests prove default command progress hides raw command text and suppresses the literal `failed` status while raw opt-in still works.
- Shared streaming tests prove status-mode command progress renders neutral lifecycle labels rather than opaque `Exec` or raw command output.
- Matrix config schema test proves `streaming.progress.commandText` and `streaming.preview.commandText` are accepted.
- Matrix docs now describe the safer default and trusted-room raw opt-in.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes` — Matrix command progress notices now hide raw command text by default and render neutral lifecycle labels instead of raw commands, opaque `Exec`, or failure-looking command-progress states.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `Yes` — Matrix streaming config now accepts `commandText` under `progress` and `preview`; default behavior is safer without requiring migration.
target.author: bdjben
